channel.title: Comments for page "A Day Notable to Nobody"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "A Day Notable to Nobody"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
expensive foil Magic: The Gathering colorless artifact card

So… Karn, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that I found the solution from SCP-4205, maybe there is a way around it. Now if Cyantreuse was still around, I could ask permission from him to try it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tis' me. I filmed on my phone, or in the case of the bar shot, Kitsun3 filmed on my phone (we were at Wasabicon and the hotel bar was convenient). For the walking shots, I used a storage unit in town. Same for the outside driving shot. And my car just looks like that. ( ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I want to know is where you got those images. I thought they were just stock footage, but then the SCP logo appeared.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm afraid there's no way around it. Shrinking the GIF files will noticeably decrease image quality and the autoplaying and looping gifs are necessary for the story. If there was a way to have autolooping videos on a page in Wikidot, maybe this could reduce the bandwidth needed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Purely technical note that the images might be a little large? They seem slow to load, and whilst I don't have the fastest connection in the world, it's still an unfamiliar experience for GIFs on a high-teens-megabit connection (read: more than 2mb/s, so if something that loops every 3-4 seconds stutters first time round it must be pretty big) vs the bad old sub-56k days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's all good. I appreciate you reading it regardless!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, I guess that’s fair enough. And that you tried to follow the prompts you got as closely as possible, making my judgement unfair. Changing to no-vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I went for the latter option in "your tale must include Halloween with an SCP Backdrop or the SCP Foundation with a Halloween backdrop." I didn't know of any way to do that without by definition of backdrop having it be a secondary element, so if that's not your thing then cool. I don't see how it's inappropriate for the contest, though, when I'm going directly off of one of the options in the requirements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, and I consider it a good tale, but. This doesn’t really feel like a Halloween tale to me, sure, there are Halloween elements, but it does not really take center stage, and is more of a “oh, it’s also Halloween btw, just so you know”

i’m considering leaving a downvote while the contest is on, and after the contest changing it back to a +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
The piece itself cannot directly acknowledge that it is in fact Halloween, but it must be blindingly obvious based on the setting and storybuilding.

The piece must start on Halloween and end during All Saints' Day sunrise.

Your tale must include Halloween with an SCP Backdrop or the SCP Foundation with a Halloween backdrop.

I felt like I was cutting it close with requirements on this, but here it is. This is goofier than anything I've done in a while, and that was hard to balance with the serious elements. I ended up cutting out a significant amount of dialogue between Trauss and 5527. Thank you to Kitsun3 and Gabriel Jade for feedback.

canon info (spoilers for other stuff from same universe):

+ show block
– hide block

In the 3848/my DoomCon team's universe, Nobody is the source of 3848, but it hasn't been stated this close-to-blatantly up until this point. Rimple's tale that's linked in this portrays the source as a giant brain-like organism growing out of control, which is true. Nobody may not be corporeal, at least not wholly. Do we really know?

Oh, and I'm sure it goes without saying, but I'm trying something new and a little weird with multimedia here. Images are of me/my car and edited by me. They are CC-by-SA-3.0. Edit: There are now four of these GIFs total, and thank you to Kitsun3 for filming the newest one (sitting down at the bar). We're currently in the tail end of Wasabicon — I wish I'd thought to do this last night when all the other cosplayers were still here, because that would have been perfect for the setting… rip
